title: Nickel Boys
logline: A young Black teenager's idealistic belief in justice is shattered when he's wrongfully sent to a brutal reform school in 1960s Florida, where he must navigate institutional racism and violence while clinging to his humanity.
antagonistOrConflict: The Nickel Academy reform school system and institutional racism

actBreaks.description: Elwood is arrested and transported to Nickel Academy, ending his life of freedom and beginning his imprisonment.

validation.ruling: Scene #41 shows Elwood in Rodney's car when they see police lights in the mirror, marking the irreversible moment that ends his normal life and forces him into the nightmare of Nickel Academy - this fundamentally changes the story's direction from his ordinary world to institutional horror.

actBreaks.description: Turner discovers Elwood's empty bed after he's taken to the White House, marking the climactic crisis of their friendship and Elwood's fate.

validation.ruling: Scene #121 shows Turner apologizing to Elwood about withholding the letter while Elwood strips an oak leaf, representing their final moment of connection before the climactic events - this marks the end of their time together at Nickel and transitions into the final act's resolution.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated non-linear structure that weaves between Elwood's youth in the 1960s and flash-forwards to his adult life, creating a haunting meditation on trauma and survival. The POV-driven narrative creates an intimate, almost documentary-like experience that places the audience directly into Elwood's perspective. The structure mirrors the fragmented nature of traumatic memory, with archival footage and images punctuating the narrative to ground it in historical reality. The pacing builds methodically through Elwood's idealistic youth, accelerates through his wrongful imprisonment, and then fragments as the brutal reality of Nickel Academy destroys his innocence.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script's pacing is deliberately contemplative in the opening act, establishing Elwood's character through quiet, observational moments. The pace quickens dramatically after scene 41 when Elwood enters Nickel, with shorter, more intense scenes reflecting the institutional brutality. The integration of archival footage creates breathing space while reinforcing the historical weight of the story.

incitingIncident.description: Elwood gets into Rodney's car, admires the dashboard, and tunes the radio while they introduce themselves.

validation.ruling: Scene #34 (photo booth) is a romantic moment but doesn't shatter Elwood's ordinary world or force irreversible action. Scene #41 shows Elwood in Rodney's car when they're pulled over by police, which directly leads to his wrongful arrest and transportation to Nickel Academy - this completely destroys his normal life and sets the main conflict in motion.

midpoint.description: Elwood awakens in the infirmary lying on his stomach as Nurse Scarlett reads Bible verses about persecution and suffering.

validation.ruling: Scene #76 shows Elwood recovering in the infirmary after his beating, which is reactive suffering rather than a proactive shift. Scene #100 depicts the boxing match where Elwood must fight Big Chet - this represents his point of no return where he can no longer avoid direct confrontation with the academy's brutal system and must actively engage rather than just endure.

Scene #143 shows Turner observing the inspectors leaving, which is setup rather than the climactic confrontation itself. Scene #149 (though omitted in the index) would represent the actual escape attempt or confrontation that resolves the main story question of whether the boys can break free from Nickel Academy's system.

scenes.heading: 1 OMITTED 1 1
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 2 OMITTED 2 2
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: EXT. CURTIS HOME - YARD - FRENCHTOWN FL - 1958 - DAY (D1) 2A
scenes.summary: Young Elwood lies in the backyard looking up at the sky and lemon tree while Evelyn and Hattie call for him.

scenes.heading: 3 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1958 - NIGHT (N2) 3 3
scenes.summary: Young Elwood sits on his mother's lap watching adults play cards at a winding-down party, focusing on a drop of condensation sliding down a beer can.

scenes.heading: 4 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1958 - NIGHT (N3) 4 4
scenes.summary: On Christmas Eve, Elwood lies under the Christmas tree watching Hattie decorate it as she hums and playfully drops an ornament that nearly hits him.

scenes.heading: 5 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1958 - DAY (D4) 5 5
scenes.summary: Elwood listens to a radio ad for FunTown amusement park offering free admission for straight-A students while looking at someone's skin up close.

scenes.heading: 6 OMITTED 6 6
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 7 EXT. PARK PLAYGROUND - TALAHASSEE FL - 1958 - DAY (D5) 7 7
scenes.summary: Elwood watches from under playground equipment as children play a game involving putting string loops around each other's tongues.

scenes.heading: 8 INT. RESTAURANT KITCHEN - HOTEL RICHMOND - TALLAHASSEE - 8 8
scenes.summary: Elwood sits in a hotel kitchen reading while watching a prep cook shuck oysters and explain the importance of looking for pearls.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - RICHMOND HOTEL - 1958 - DAY (D7) 8A
scenes.summary: Hattie playfully traps Elwood under bedsheets while making a hotel bed, pretending she hears mice when he giggles.

scenes.heading: 9 INT. CURTIS HOME - HATTIE’S BEDROOM - 1958 - NIGHT (N8) 9 9
scenes.summary: Elwood sneaks into Hattie's bedroom at night and discovers a machete under her pillow, startling her awake when he calls her name.

scenes.heading: 10 OMITTED 10 10
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: INT. LAUNDRY ROOM - HOTEL RICHMOND - 1958 - DAY (D9) 10A
scenes.summary: Elwood watches Hattie iron hotel linens in the laundry room, seeing his reflection in the chrome base of the iron.

scenes.heading: 11 INT. CURTIS HOME - HALLWAY/BA THROOM - 1958 - EVENING (E9) 11 11
scenes.summary: Elwood quietly observes Hattie relaxing in a bath, noticing her worn knees from years of scrubbing floors before carefully leaving.

scenes.heading: 12 EXT. TALLAHASSEE STREET - SIDEWALK - 1965 - DAY (D10) 12 12
scenes.summary: Teenage Elwood watches TVs go to static in a store window while women on the street praise Hattie for raising such a fine boy and discuss recent events in Selma.

scenes.heading: 13 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1965 - DAY (D11) 13 13
scenes.summary: Elwood stands in the living room watching the hair on his arm rise into goose bumps and then flatten, adjusting his arm to find the best angle.

scenes.heading: 14 OMITTED 14 14
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. FRENCHTOWN STREET - CITY BUS - 1966 - DAY (D12) 15 15
scenes.summary: Elwood rides the city bus and watches three Black teenage boys on the sidewalk step aside for an older white couple walking toward them.

scenes.heading: 16 INT. MARCONI’S TOBACCO - FRENCHTOWN - 1966 - DAY (D13) 16 16
scenes.summary: Elwood reads comics in Marconi's tobacco shop while Mr. Marconi arranges cigars and discusses the importance of order and packaging with his wife.

scenes.heading: 17 INT. LINCOLN H.S. CLASSROOM - TALLAHASSEE (1966) - DAY 17 17
scenes.summary: On the first day of class, Mr. Hill hands out black markers for students to cross out racial slurs left in their used textbooks.

scenes.heading: 18 OMITTED 18 18
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: INT. CITY BUS - FRENCHTOWN - 1966 - DAY (D15) 18A
scenes.summary: Elwood rides the bus with Hattie and plays with a little girl who slides under the seats between passengers' feet.

scenes.heading: 19 INT. MARCONI'S TOBACCO SHOP - 1966 - DAY (D16) 19 19
scenes.summary: Elwood watches two well-dressed women, one Black and one white, browse Marconi's shop in synchronized movements.

scenes.heading: 20 INT. CURTIS HOME - ELWOOD’S BEDROOM - 1966 - DAY (D17) 20 20
scenes.summary: Elwood examines the newly discovered hair in his armpit, twisting and pulling it while sniffing to explore this change in his body.

scenes.heading: 21 EXT. FRENCHTOWN STREET - 1966 - DAY (D18) 21 21
scenes.summary: Elwood waits at a crosswalk and follows a family across the street, focusing on a girl named Simone as they walk faster than him.

scenes.heading: 22 INT. MARCONI'S TOBACCO SHOP - 1966 - DAY (D19) 22 22
scenes.summary: Elwood sits on a step-ladder in Marconi's examining his palm lines while reading a Silver Surfer comic with LIFE magazine showing protest images on his lap.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDEWALK - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY (D20) 22A
scenes.summary: Elwood stands in a police lineup with other Black teenage boys as an old white man searches them with a cane while a police officer watches.

scenes.heading: 23 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1966 - DAY (D21) 23 23
scenes.summary: Elwood lies on the couch playing with a balloon to keep it from hitting the ceiling fan while watching TV coverage of a NASA rocket launch.

scenes.heading: 24 INT. LINCOLN H.S. - CLASSROOM - 1966 - DAY (D22) 24 24
scenes.summary: Mr. Hill tries to find the right spot on a Dr. King speech record to play for his class as students return from recess.

scenes.heading: 25 EXT. SIDEWALK - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY (D22) 25 25
scenes.summary: Elwood waits at a bus stop watching a suit hanging outside a parked car dance in the breeze created by passing headlights.

scenes.heading: 26 OMITTED 26 26
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 27 EXT. ABANDONED ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY 27 27
scenes.summary: Elwood attends a civil rights meeting in an abandoned school where an older activist explains they're breaking the chain reaction of evil.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elwood's first direct involvement with civil rights activism

scenes.heading: 28 EXT. SIDEWALK - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY (D24) 28 28
scenes.summary: Elwood finds a two-headed penny on the sidewalk that shows tails on both sides no matter how many times he flips it.

scenes.heading: INT. PHOTO BOOTH - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY 28A
scenes.summary: Elwood and Simone hold hands and kiss in a photo booth while taking pictures together.

scenes.heading: EXT. PHOTO BOOTH - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY 28B
scenes.summary: Elwood watches the strip of photos of him and Simone emerge from the photo booth slot.

scenes.heading: 29 INT. CURTIS HOME - KITCHEN - 1966 - DAY (D25) 29 29
scenes.summary: Elwood sits at the kitchen table watching a pamphlet slide down the refrigerator while Hattie talks on the phone about protests and newspaper coverage.

scenes.heading: 30 INT. MARCONI'S TOBACCO SHOP - 1966 - DAY (D26) 30 30
scenes.summary: Elwood catches two neighborhood boys stealing comics at Marconi's and tells them to put the items back, earning a menacing look from one of them.

scenes.heading: 31 EXT. SIDEWALK - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - NIGHT (N26) 31 31
scenes.summary: Elwood walks nervously down the street at night, constantly looking over his shoulder, when he suddenly stops to watch a large alligator cross the street.

scenes.heading: 32 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1966 - NIGHT (N26) 32 32
scenes.summary: Hattie tends to Elwood's injuries with an ice pack, fighting back tears as she sees he's been beaten up.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elwood suffers violent retaliation, escalating the consequences of his activism

scenes.heading: 33 EXT. RURAL ROAD - NEAR TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY - (D27) 33 33
scenes.summary: Elwood hitchhikes on a country road when Rodney pulls up in a flashy emerald Chevy Impala and offers him a ride.

scenes.heading: 34 INT. CHEVY CAR - TALAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY (D27) 34 34
scenes.summary: Elwood gets into Rodney's car, admires the dashboard, and tunes the radio while they introduce themselves.

scenes.heading: 35 INT. CURTIS HOME - ELWOOD’S BEDROOM - 1966 - DAY (D28) 35 35
scenes.summary: Elwood sits motionless in his bedroom with a string wound tightly around his finger while Hattie talks desperately on the phone to police about his innocence.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elwood faces arrest and the collapse of his future

scenes.heading: 36 INT. CURTIS HOME - DOWNSTAIRS HALLWAY - 1966 - DAY (D29) 36 36
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood in 2018 looks at computer images of unmarked graves discovered through ground-penetrating radar technology.

scenes.heading: 37 INT. CURTIS HOME - ELWOOD’S BEDROOM - 1966 - DAY (D30) 37 37
scenes.summary: Elwood walks downstairs in pajamas to find Hattie disoriented and frantically searching through kitchen cabinets while calling his name.

scenes.heading: 38 EXT. PARK - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY (D31) 38 38
scenes.summary: Elwood lies in bed staring at the window which seems unusually far away as sunlight moves across the room and Mr. Hill calls for him from downstairs.

scenes.heading: 39 OMITTED 39 39
scenes.summary: Elwood sits alone on a park bench in despair, watching his shadow disappear and reappear as clouds pass over the sun.

scenes.heading: 40 INT. CURTIS HOME - DINING AREA - 1966 - EVENING (E32) 40 40
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. POLICE CAR - RURAL ROAD - FLORIDA - 1966 - DAY (D33) 41 41
scenes.summary: Hattie cuts a lopsided cake while telling the story of her father's lynching, explaining how acting above your station leads to punishment.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hattie reveals the family's traumatic history with racial violence, contextualizing Elwood's fate

scenes.heading: 42 INT. ADULT ELWOOD’S APARTMENT - NYC - 2018 - DAY (D-FF1) 42 42
scenes.summary: Elwood rides in the back of a police car with two other teenage boys while a corrections officer eats fried chicken and drives them toward their destination.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elwood is transported to reform school, his punishment now in motion

scenes.heading: 43 INT. POLICE CAR - NICKEL ACADEMY - MARIANNA FL - 1966 - 43 43
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood looks at computer images of unmarked graves discovered through ground-penetrating radar.

scenes.heading: 44 OMITTED 44 44
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: INT. INTAKE BUILDING - NICKEL ACADEMY - 1966 - DAY (D33) 44A
scenes.summary: Elwood sits in the run-down intake room as Spencer explains the four behavioral ranks at Nickel Academy to twelve Black boys.

scenes.heading: 45 INT. ADULT ELWOOD'S APARTMENT - NYC - 2018 - NIGHT (N-FF2) 45 45
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood searches online for information about unmarked graves discovered at the former Nickel Academy while his wife calls from another room.

scenes.heading: 46 OMITTED 46 46
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 47 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1966 - DAY (D33) 47 47
scenes.summary: House Father Blakeley assigns Elwood to an empty bed in the dormitory among thirty beds filled with boys in threadbare uniforms.

scenes.heading: 48 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE 48 48
scenes.summary: Silent archival footage shows well-dressed Black boys in an orderly reform school classroom setting.

scenes.heading: 49 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - SHOWERS - 1966 - DAY (D34) 49 49
scenes.summary: Blakeley gives Elwood two minutes in the freezing shower, causing him to shiver and breathe heavily.

scenes.heading: 50 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DAY (D35) 50 50
scenes.summary: Elwood walks between buildings and watches a tractor pulling exhausted boys returning from field work.

scenes.heading: 51 OMITTED 51 51
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 52 ARCHIVAL IMAGE 52 52
scenes.summary: Archival images show boys from the actual Dozier School, followed by time-lapse footage from inside a railway boxcar traveling north.

scenes.heading: 53 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DAY (D35) 53 53
scenes.summary: Elwood watches white boys playing football on the other side of campus when Spencer appears and stops their game.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DINING HALL - 1966 - MORNING (D35B) 54 54
scenes.summary: Elwood struggles to find a seat in the loud dining hall as older boys and younger boys both reject him from their tables.

scenes.heading: 55 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - CLASSROOM - 1966 - DAY (D35B) 55 55
scenes.summary: Elwood stares at abandoned terrariums and a cloudy aquarium in the classroom when someone throws a paper ball into the water.

scenes.heading: 56 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1966 - NIGHT (D35B) 56 56
scenes.summary: Elwood lies in his bunk listening to mysterious roaring sounds outside as Black Mike lights a match across the room.

scenes.heading: 57 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE 57 57
scenes.summary: Archival footage shows the opening scene of 'The Defiant Ones' with Sidney Poitier singing.

scenes.heading: 58 OMITTED 58 58
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - CLASSROOM - 1966 - DAY (D36) 59 59
scenes.summary: Elwood sits in a crowded classroom while the teacher explains basic math and Griff's group plays cards in the back.

scenes.heading: 60 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - BATHROOM - 1966 - AFTERNOON (D37) 60 60
scenes.summary: Elwood intervenes when Black Mike corners and threatens Corey in the bathroom stall, stepping between them.

scenes.heading: 61 ARCHIVAL IMAGES 61 61
scenes.summary: Silent archival images show exhumed student graves and their contents including marbles, buttons, and personal items.

scenes.heading: 62 EXT. CITRUS GROVE - NICKEL PLANTATIONS - 1966 - DAY (D38) 62 62
scenes.summary: Elwood works in the citrus grove picking oranges while men on stilts supervise the boys from above.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1966- NIGHT (N38) 63 63
scenes.summary: Earl and white guards wake Elwood in his bunk and take him along with Black Mike and Corey from the dormitory.

scenes.heading: 64 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - “WHITE HOUSE” - 1966 - NIGHT (N38) 64 64
scenes.summary: Spencer leads the boys in darkness to the White House building, unlocking two padlocks to enter.

scenes.heading: 65 INT. “WHITE HOUSE” - 1966 - NIGHT [CONTINUOUS] (N38) 65 65
scenes.summary: The boys enter the White House and smell urine, feces, and fear as Spencer takes Black Mike into the beating room first.

scenes.heading: 66 INT. “WHITE HOUSE” - BEATING ROOM - 1966 - NIGHT [CONT] 66 66
scenes.summary: Elwood sees the industrial fan that creates the roaring sound and fresh blood splatter on the walls as Spencer calls his name.

scenes.heading: 67 ARCHIVAL IMAGES 67 67
scenes.summary: Still portraits of children and their adult selves change with each sound of the whip as Hattie's voice calls to Elwood.

scenes.heading: 68 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - INFIRMARY - 1966 - DAY (D39) 68 68
scenes.summary: Elwood awakens in the infirmary lying on his stomach as Nurse Scarlett reads Bible verses about persecution and suffering.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elwood survives his brutal beating at the White House, marking his full initiation into Nickel's violence

scenes.heading: 69 EXT. NICKEL FIELD - COMMUNITY SERVICE VAN - 1966 - DAY 69 69
scenes.summary: Turner sits in a community service van as Harper playfully pops his bubble gum and smacks his mouth.

scenes.heading: 70 OMITTED 70 70
scenes.summary: Silent archival footage shows two young Black brothers playing in a suburban yard with a white picket fence.

scenes.heading: 71 EXT. NICKEL - COMMUNITY SERVICE VAN - 1966 - DAY (D40) 71 71
scenes.summary: Turner sees Jaime waiting anxiously at a picnic table and a white guard with his arm around another boy's shoulder.

scenes.heading: 72 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE 72 72
scenes.summary: Archival footage placeholder marked as 'TBD'.

scenes.heading: 73 EXT. MARIANNA FL - BACK ALLEY - 1966 - DAY (D41) 73 73
scenes.summary: Turner watches a butcher pass an envelope of cash to Harper through the van window in a back alley.

scenes.heading: 74 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - INFIRMARY - 1966 - DAY (D42) 74 74
scenes.summary: Elwood lies in the infirmary as Nurse Scarlett tends to other patients but ignores his waste-filled bedpan.

scenes.heading: 75 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DAY (D43) 75 75
scenes.summary: Hattie approaches Turner on campus asking about Elwood while a white boy operates a loud lawnmower nearby.

scenes.heading: 76 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - INFIRMARY - 1966 - DAY (D43) 76 76
scenes.summary: Turner visits Elwood in the infirmary and explains the four ways to leave Nickel while Elwood watches Doctor Cooke at the medicine cabinet.
scenes.turningPointNote: Turner and Elwood's partnership begins as Turner offers guidance and protection

scenes.heading: 77 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE - 1960S 77 77
scenes.summary: Silent archival footage shows a Black man running a shell game on a city sidewalk with gamblers and onlookers.

scenes.heading: 78 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - STAIRWELL - 1966 - DAY (D44) 78 78
scenes.summary: Griff body-checks Elwood hard on the stairwell, spinning him around, but nods with respect when Elwood says he's not scared.

scenes.heading: 79 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - BEHIND A BUILDING - 1966 - DAY (D45) 79 79
scenes.summary: Elwood sits alone behind a building, weaving gauze bandages around his fingers in strange shapes.

scenes.heading: 80 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - SMALL GYMNASIUM - 1966 - DAY (D46) 80 80
scenes.summary: Turner watches Griff pummel Black Mike in the boxing ring until Black Mike collapses and Griff spits out his mouthpiece.

scenes.heading: 81 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DAY (D47) 81 81
scenes.summary: Turner approaches Blakeley and tells him Elwood has been reassigned from field work to community service.

scenes.heading: 82 INT. COMMUNITY SERVICE VAN - NICKEL ACADEMY - 1966 - DAY 82 82
scenes.summary: Elwood, Turner, and Harper sit in the community service van filled with government supplies as Harper explains their mission.

scenes.heading: 83 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - INTAKE BUILDING - 1966 - DAY (D48) 83 83
scenes.summary: Elwood follows an alligator to the intake building and overhears Spencer giving his orientation speech to new boys.

scenes.heading: 84 OMITTED 84 84
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 85 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - REC ROOM - 1966 - DAY (D49) 85 85
scenes.summary: Elwood writes in a notebook from the van while Turner reads comics and Black Mike intimidates him by flipping Elwood's penny.

scenes.heading: INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1966 - DAY (D49) 85A
scenes.summary: Turner walks along floor tiles like a sobriety test while joking with Elwood and Jaime about Blakeley's drinking.

scenes.heading: 86 OMITTED 86 86
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted, followed by repeated archival footage of two Black brothers playing in a suburban yard.

scenes.heading: 87 OMITTED 87 87
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - REC ROOM - 1966 - DAY (D49) 87A
scenes.summary: A small lizard runs across the ping pong table and crawls up a boy's pants in the rec room.

scenes.heading: 88 EXT. NICKEL - GROUNDS - 1966 - LATE AFTERNOON (D50) 88 88
scenes.summary: Turner tells Elwood about his Aunt Mavis and her abusive boyfriend while they sit peacefully on the campus lawn.

scenes.heading: 89 OMITTED 89 89
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted, followed by archival footage of a Florida tourism commercial showing happy white people.

scenes.heading: 90 EXT. HARDEE HOUSE - PORCH - MARIANNA - 1966 - DAY (D51) 90 90
scenes.summary: Turner and Elwood paint the porch of Director Hardee's pink house while Mrs. Hardee brings them orange juice and talks about the property's history.

scenes.heading: 91 INT. NICKEL - BARN - STALL/HIDING PLACE - 1966 - DAY (D52) 91 91
scenes.summary: Elwood reads a book about a British mother trying to marry off her daughter while Turner listens to a ball game in their secret hiding place in the barn.

scenes.heading: 92 OMITTED 92 92
scenes.summary: Archival footage shows two children playing.

scenes.heading: 93 EXT. HARDEE HOUSE - POOL - 1966 - DAY (D53) 93 93
scenes.summary: Turner swims underwater in Mrs. Hardee's pool while she sits poolside and tells Elwood to use the shallow end, then quickly gets up and walks away.

scenes.heading: 94 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1966 - DAY (D54) 94 94
scenes.summary: Blakeley arrives tipsy for mail call and snatches Elwood's envelope back just as he's about to take it.

scenes.heading: 95 OMITTED 95 95
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 96 EXT. NICKEL - BOOT HILL - 1966 - DAY (D55) 96 96
scenes.summary: Turner shows Elwood the iron restraint rings grown into trees on Boot Hill, with ants crawling on them.

scenes.heading: 97 INT. NICKEL - BARN - STALL/HIDING PLACE - 1966 - DAY (D56) 97 97
scenes.summary: Elwood and Turner play a game about things they've lost when Spencer enters the barn and tells Griff that good sportsmanship sometimes means letting the other team win.

scenes.heading: 98 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - CLASSROOM - 1966 - DAY (D57) 98 98
scenes.summary: Elwood watches an ant march along the window sill and looks out at the white boys' side of campus.

scenes.heading: 99 OMITTED 99 99
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 100 INT. NICKEL - SMALL GYMNASIUM - BOXING MATCH - 1966 - 100 100
scenes.summary: A boxing match is underway in the gymnasium with segregated seating as Harper forcibly moves a light-skinned boy to the white side.
scenes.turningPointNote: The boxing match represents the climactic confrontation that Spencer has been orchestrating, with the forced segregation highlighting the institutional control and manipulation at Nickel.

scenes.heading: 101 INT. RESTAURANT KITCHEN - HARLEM - 1968 - NIGHT (N-FF3) 101 101
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood washes dishes in a Harlem restaurant kitchen while humming and watching rainbows form in the dishwater.

scenes.heading: 102 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DUSK (D59) 102 102
scenes.summary: Black boys decorate a tall tree with Christmas tinsel and lights in a slow spiral around its trunk.

scenes.heading: 103 OMITTED 103 103
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 104 OMITTED 104 104
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 105 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE - 1960S 105 105
scenes.summary: Archival footage shows Black boys opening Christmas presents while Apollo 8 astronauts describe the moon's foreboding horizon.

scenes.heading: 106 INT. RESTAURANT KITCHEN - HARLEM - 1968 - NIGHT (N-FF3) 106 106
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood watches a bubble grow in the dishwater sink as lunar broadcast static continues in the background.

scenes.heading: 107 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE - 1968 107 107
scenes.summary: Apollo 8 astronauts take turns reading Genesis while orbiting the moon.

scenes.heading: 108 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DAY (D60) 108 108
scenes.summary: Turner observes the torso of a broken papier-mâché Santa Claus lying at the foot of a tree.

scenes.heading: 109 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - NIGHT (N60) 109 109
scenes.summary: Elwood waits by a tree as Christmas lights are turned on across campus and Turner joins him to watch.

scenes.heading: 110 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D61) 110 110
scenes.summary: Hattie visits Elwood on visiting day, looking years older and thinner, while Corey and his mother join their table.

scenes.heading: 111 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D61) 111 111
scenes.summary: Turner apologizes to Elwood for withholding a letter as Elwood methodically tears apart an oak leaf.

scenes.heading: 112 INT. WIDOWER APARTMENT - NEW YORK - 1975 - DAY (D-FF4) 112 112
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood and Larry move a bed frame and discover a faded leaf outline pressed into the floor beneath it.

scenes.heading: 113 OMITTED 113 113
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 114 OMITTED 114 114
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 115 EXT. STREET [UNDER PORTICO] - MARIANNA - 1967 - DAY (D62) 115 115
scenes.summary: Elwood and Turner walk freely in Marianna while white townspeople eye them suspiciously in their state-issued uniforms.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN STREET SIDEWALK - MARIANNA - 1967 - DAY (D62) 115A
scenes.summary: Elwood suddenly darts across traffic toward a cardboard cutout of Martin Luther King Jr. being carried away by a white man, dropping his notebook in the road.

scenes.heading: 116 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - REC ROOM - 1967 - DAY (D63) 116 116
scenes.summary: Turner punches Elwood with his own fist during a chess game, causing Elwood to drop two black knight pieces.

scenes.heading: 117 INT. LIVING AREA - TENEMENT APARTMENT - NYC - 1975 - DUSK 117 117
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood watches TV with girlfriend Denise in their tenement apartment as she tells him about being scared by a rat.

scenes.heading: 118 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE 118 118
scenes.summary: The Defiant Ones plays on TV showing Sidney Poitier and Tony Curtis as chained convicts arguing about escape.

scenes.heading: 119 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE - 1970S 119 119
scenes.summary: Archival footage shows Black boys playing chess in Washington Square Park.

scenes.heading: 120 INT. COMMUNITY SERVICE VAN - GROUNDS - NICKEL - 1967 - 120 120
scenes.summary: Elwood calls after Turner as he walks away from the Community Service van at a padlocked storehouse, ignoring him.

scenes.heading: 121 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1967 - NIGHT (N65) 121 121
scenes.summary: Turner awakens to find a grown man climbing into Elwood's bed, then crawling away as Elwood replaces him.
scenes.turningPointNote: This scene reveals the sexual abuse occurring at Nickel, representing a crucial moment of horror and understanding for Turner about what Elwood is enduring.

scenes.heading: 122 OMITTED 122 122
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 123 INT. BAR - NEW YORK - 1988 - DAY (D-FF6) 123 123
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood watches marathon runners on TV in a bar when Adult Chickie Pete recognizes him and approaches.

scenes.heading: 124 OMITTED 124 124
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 125 INT. NICKEL - INTAKE BUILDING - 1967 - DAY (D66) 125 125
scenes.summary: Boys line up to get fresh uniforms for an inspection while Blakeley warns them about Spencer's expectations.

scenes.heading: 126 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D66) 126 126
scenes.summary: Turner walks around a building being painted and sees three identical paint-covered Black boys who don't appear to notice him.

scenes.heading: 127 INT. ADULT ELWOOD’S APARTMENT - NY - 2018 - DAY (D-FF7) 127 127
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood looks at a newspaper photo on his computer showing Spencer as an elderly 'Good Citizen of the Year.'

scenes.heading: 128 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D66) 128 128
scenes.summary: Turner brings tools to Harper when a state photographer suddenly takes flash pictures of them.

scenes.heading: 129 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DINING HALL - 1967 - DAY (D66) 129 129
scenes.summary: Boys enjoy an unusually good meal during the state inspection while Turner sits with untouched food, viewing it as an abomination.

scenes.heading: 130 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D66) 130 130
scenes.summary: Turner observes a ragged Black boy stumbling down from Boot Hill, standing still and looking up at the sky.

scenes.heading: 131 EXT. NICKEL - FIELD - BLEACHERS - 1967 - DAY (D66) 131 131
scenes.summary: Elwood hammers nails on scaffold repairs when inspectors arrive, causing him to knock over a box of nails that rain down on Desmond below.

scenes.heading: 132 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D66) 132 132
scenes.summary: Turner watches from the van as the state inspectors and Hardee laugh together in a choreographed good ole boy routine after completing their inspection.
scenes.turningPointNote: Turner realizes this is his moment to act, understanding the corruption is complete and systematic - this drives him toward his escape attempt.

scenes.heading: EXT. CURTIS HOME - FRENCHTOWN - 1967 - DAY (D-FF8) 132A
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood knocks on Hattie's door and she opens it wide after initially peeking through the chain, trembling with emotion at seeing him.

scenes.heading: 133 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - BOOT HILL - 1967 - DAY (D66) 133 133
scenes.summary: Elwood walks slowly through Boot Hill past the iron restraints, whistling a gospel song for courage as leaves spiral in the wind.

scenes.heading: 134 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - SCHOOL HALLWAY - 1967 - DAY (D66) 134 134
scenes.summary: Turner encounters a skinny mule with straw in its mouth in the school hallway and touches it to confirm it's real.

scenes.heading: 135 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1967 - DUSK (D66) 135 135
scenes.summary: Jaime warns Turner that they will take Elwood tonight, revealing he was captured right after lunch and is being held upstairs.

scenes.heading: 136 INT. NICKEL - ATTIC SWEAT BOX - 1967 - DUSK (D66) 136 136
scenes.summary: Elwood sits in darkness in the attic sweatbox as vibrations and screaming sounds escalate into a roaring like a falling passenger jet.

scenes.heading: 137 OMITTED 137 137
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 138 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE 138 138
scenes.summary: Archival footage shows mules and men toppling a colossal Southern tree.

scenes.heading: 139 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - SWEAT BOX - 1967 - NIGHT (N66) 139 139
scenes.summary: Turner opens the sweat box and finds Elwood's weakened head emerging from the darkness after his confinement.

scenes.heading: 140 INT. NICKEL - HALLWAY - 1967 - NIGHT (N66) 140 140
scenes.summary: Turner watches as the dehydrated and stiff Elwood struggles to put on his arrival shirt in preparation for escape.

scenes.heading: 141 INT. NICKEL - ANOTHER HALLWAY - 1967 - NIGHT (N66) 141 141
scenes.summary: Turner and Elwood creep through hallways past an office where Spencer, Blakeley, and Earl play poker, discussing card strategy.

scenes.heading: 142 OMITTED 142 142
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 143 EXT. HARDEE HOUSE - 1967 - NIGHT (N66) 143 143
scenes.summary: Turner and Elwood move along the Hardee house pool as Turner explains they had to escape tonight because Spencer planned to kill Elwood.
scenes.turningPointNote: The escape attempt begins with the revelation that Elwood's life is in immediate danger

scenes.heading: 144 EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - NEAR MARIANNA - DAWN INTO DAY (D67) 144 144
scenes.summary: Turner and Elwood ride bicycles on a desolate country road at dawn when a red pickup truck passes them.

scenes.heading: EXT. DIRT ROAD - CANES FIELDS - MORNING (D67) 144A
scenes.summary: Turner rides joyfully through cane fields until he sees the Community Service Van approaching behind the smiling Elwood.

scenes.heading: EXT. FURROWED CANE FIELD - MORNING [CONTINUOUS] (D67) 144B
scenes.summary: Turner runs into a scorched cane field while Elwood drops his bike and flees as the van stops and Harper chases with a rifle, firing shots.
scenes.turningPointNote: The escape reaches its climax with armed pursuit and gunfire

scenes.heading: 145 OMITTED 145 145
scenes.summary: Archival footage shows a prison escape with white men in uniform gathering bloodhounds and mounting horses for pursuit.

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL LANDSCAPE - FLORIDA - DUSK (D67) 145B
scenes.summary: Turner runs through grassy landscape at dusk while archival footage continues showing the prison chase with bloodhounds and military planes.

scenes.heading: 146 OMITTED 146 146
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 147 EXT. RURAL LANDSCAPE - FLORIDA - NIGHT (N67) 147 147
scenes.summary: Turner continues running through darkness and night, slowing down then starting up again.

scenes.heading: 148 OMITTED 148 148
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 149 OMITTED 149 149
scenes.summary: A rapid montage of archival images including space shuttles, brain scans, license plate machines, electric chair viewing rooms, and Black families.

scenes.heading: EXT. CURTIS HOME - FRENCHTOWN FL - 1967 -DAY (D-FF8) W150
scenes.summary: Hattie opens her door to Turner and backs away trembling and shaking her head, realizing from his presence that Elwood is dead.
scenes.turningPointNote: The devastating revelation of Elwood's death is delivered to his grandmother

scenes.heading: INT. ADULT ELWOOD’S APARTMENT - 2002 - NYC
scenes.summary: Adult Turner sits on a couch flipping channels while his wife Millie films him with a new digital camcorder.

scenes.heading: INT. ADULT ELWOOD'S APARTMENT - 2002 - NYC
scenes.summary: Adult Turner wakes to find Millie filming him, they laugh together and look lovingly into the camcorder monitor while archival footage shows Hattie's death notice.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that Turner has been living as Elwood, with Hattie's death notice confirming the identity switch

scenes.heading: EXT. NICKEL GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY YY150
scenes.summary: Turner looks up from an open grave at a tree canopy as Elwood appears above, laughs, and reaches down to clasp Turner's hand and pull him out.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final scene reveals the spiritual connection between the friends, suggesting redemption and transcendence
